title: aegilops squarrosa subsp. strangulata
intro: Aegilops squarrosa subsp. strangulata, also known as Strangled Aegilops or Strangled Brome, is a rare and lesser-known subspecies of the Aegilops squarrosa plant, a type of wild grass native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been studied for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Aegilops squarrosa subsp. strangulata include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and decoctions, can be used to harness its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects on human health.
